memes.label: "Introduction"
memes.description: "Any editor of Slav folktales starts with great advantages."

memes.label: "The Pronunciation of Russian Words"
memes.description: "Every Russian word has *one* strongly accented syllable, which is marked with an acute accent. The vowels are to be sounded as in Italian."

memes.label: "Russian Folktales"
memes.description: "本结构包含以下章节："

memes.label: "The Dun Cow"
memes.description: "You know that there are all sorts in this world, good and bad, people who do not fear God, and feel no shame before their own brother."

memes.label: "A Tale of the Dead"
memes.description: "One day a peasant was going by night with pots on his head. He journeyed on and on, and his horse became tired and came to a spot in front of God’s acre."

memes.label: "A Tale of the Dead"
memes.description: "Once a carpenter was going home late at night from a strange village: he had been at a jolly feast at a friend’s house."

memes.label: "A Tale of the Dead"
memes.description: "They had discharged the soldier home, and he was going on his road, it may be far, it may be a short way, and he at last was nearing his village."

memes.label: "The Bear, the Dog, and the Cat"
memes.description: "Once there lived a peasant who had a good dog, and as the dog grew old it left off barking and guarding the yard and the storehouses: its master would no longer nourish it, so t."

memes.label: "Egóri the Brave and the Gipsy"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom, in a certain land, there was a gipsy who had a wife and seven children, and he lived so poorly that at last there was nothing in the house to eat or to dri."

memes.label: "Danílo the Unfortunate"
memes.description: "Good Prince Vladímir had many henchmen and serfs in the city of Kíev, and amongst them there was Danílo the Unfortunate, the noble."

memes.label: "The Sorry Drunkard"
memes.description: "Once there lived an old man, and he was such a sorry drunkard as words cannot describe."

memes.label: "The Wolf and the Tailor"
memes.description: "This story is a story of the past—of the days when Christ and the Twelve Apostles still walked on earth.16"

memes.label: "The Tale of the Silver Saucer and the Crystal Apple"
memes.description: "Once a peasant lived with his wife, and they had three daughters: two were finely dressed and clever, but the third was a simple girl; the sisters and the father and mother as w."

memes.label: "The Foundling Prince"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a Tsar and Tsarítsa who had only one son. The Tsar one day had to leave home, and in his absence a disaster befell them; the Tsarévich disappeared."

memes.label: "The Sun and How It Was Made by Divine Will"
memes.description: "The Sun is thirty times the size it appears: looks very small because it is very high up from the earth."

memes.label: "The Language of the Birds"
memes.description: "In a certain city there was a merchant and his wife and their son, who was wise beyond his years; he was called Vasíli."

memes.label: "Bába Yagá and Zamorýshek"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there lived an old man and his old wife, and they had no children, and what on earth did they not do to get them! How did not they beseech God! But for all that."

memes.label: "The Miraculous Hen"
memes.description: "Beyond thrice-nine lands, in the thrice-tenth realm—it was not in our kingdom—once an old man and an old woman lived in great need and poverty."

memes.label: "Mark the Rich"
memes.description: "In a country, in a kingdom far away, once upon a time there lived a merchant, Mark the Rich; and, what with all his estates and revenues, you couldn’t count them."

memes.label: "By Command of the Prince Daniel"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was an aged queen who had a son and a daughter, who were fine, sturdy children."

memes.label: "The Thoughtless Word"
memes.description: "Once upon a time an old man lived in a village with his wife, and they were very poor: they had only one son."

memes.label: "The Tsarítsa Harpist"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom in a certain land once there lived a Tsar and a Tsarítsa."

memes.label: "The Tale of Iván Tsarévich, the Bird of Light, and the Grey Wolf"
memes.description: "There was once, in a certain kingdom, a certain state, where there lived Tsar Výslav Andrónovich, who had three sons: the first was called Dmítri Tsarévich, the second Vasíli Ts."

memes.label: "The Priest with the Envious Eyes"
memes.description: "There was once a priest who lived in the parish of St. Nicholas. He served St."

memes.label: "The Soldier and Death"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a soldier who had served God and the Great Sovereign for twenty-five whole years, and had only in the end earned three biscuits, and was journeying ba."

memes.label: "The Midnight Dance"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a king who was a widower. He had twelve daughters: each was fairer than the others."

memes.label: "Vasilísa the Fair"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a merchant who had been married for twelve years and had only one daughter, Vasilísa the Fair. When her mother died the girl was eight years old."

memes.label: "The Animals in the Pit"
memes.description: "A Pig was going to church at St. Petersburg, and the Wolf met him."

memes.label: "The Poor Widow"
memes.description: "A very long time ago Christ and the twelve Apostles walked on earth. They went about like simple people, and nobody could have known that it was Christ and the twelve Apostles."

memes.label: "Ilyá Múromets and Svyatogór the Knight"
memes.description: "From the famous city of Múrom, out of the village of Karachárovo, the valiant, doughty youth Ilyá Múromets, the son of Iván, set out far into the open fields."

memes.label: "The Smith and the Devil"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a smith who had a son six years old—a sturdy and sensible lad."

memes.label: "The Princess Who Would Not Smile"
memes.description: "If you think of it, what a big world God’s world is: in it rich and poor folk live, and there is room enough for them all; and the Lord overlooks and judges them all."

memes.label: "The Tsarévich and Dyád’ka"
memes.description: "Once upon a time, in a certain kingdom, in a city of yore, there was a King who had a dwarf son. The Tsarévich was fair to behold, and fair of heart."

memes.label: "Prince Evstáfi"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom once there lived a Tsar who had a young son—Tsarévich Evstáfi—who did not love visiting or dances, nor promenades, but only liked going in the streets and w."

memes.label: "Vasilísa Popóvna"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom, in a certain country, once there lived Vasíli the pope and his daughter, Vasilísa Vasílyevna."

memes.label: "The Dream"
memes.description: "One day an old, old man was wandering about the earth, and he asked for a night’s shelter from the peasant."

memes.label: "The Soldier and the Tsar in the Forest"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom, in a certain State, lived a peasant who had two sons. The recruiting-sergeant came round and took the elder brother."

memes.label: "The Tale of Alexander of Macedon"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there lived a king on the earth whose name was Alexander of Macedon: this was in the old days very long ago."

memes.label: "The Brother of Christ"
memes.description: "An old man was dying, and he was enjoining on his son not to forget the poor."

memes.label: "Alyósha Popóvich"
memes.description: "In the sky the young bright moon was being born, and on the earth, of the old prebendary, the old pope León, a son was born, a mighty knight, and he was called by name Alyósha P."

memes.label: "God’s Blessing Compasses All Things"
memes.description: "Once upon a time in a certain country, in a certain kingdom, there were two peasants, Iván and Naúm."

memes.label: "Shemyák the Judge"
memes.description: "Once in a certain country, in a certain kingdom, there lived two brothers; one was rich and the other poor."

memes.label: "A Story of Saint Nicholas"
memes.description: "In a certain city, in a certain state, there once lived a merchant Nicholas with his wife. From the beginning they lived happily and were wealthy."

memes.label: "The Potter"
memes.description: "Once a potter was journeying on his road with his goods and dozed off. The Tsar Iván Vasílyevich came driving by in his carriage and said, “Peace be to you!”"

memes.label: "The Witch and the Sister of the Sun"
memes.description: "In a distant country, a country far away, once there lived a Tsar and Tsarítsa, who had a son, Iván Tsarévich, who was dumb from his birth."

memes.label: "Márya Moryévna"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom, in a certain state, there once lived Iván Tsarévich, who had three sisters: one was called Márya Tsarévna, the second Ólga Tsarévna, and the third Ánna Tsa."

memes.label: "The Realm of Stone"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom, in a certain state, once there lived a soldier who had served long and faithfully and knew all about the Tsar’s service, the reviews, and always came up to."

memes.label: "The Story of Tsar Angéy and How He Suffered for Pride"
memes.description: "Once there was in the city of Filuyán34 a Tsar named Angéy, who was very famous."

memes.label: "The Feast of the Dead"
memes.description: "Some girls were out at night for the evening, and arranged for an evening party. They went out to get some vodka. There were bones lying on the road."

memes.label: "The Quarrelsome Wife"
memes.description: "“Father, I should like to marry! Mother, I should like to marry, I should really,” said the youth."

memes.label: "Elijah the Prophet and St. Nicholas"
memes.description: "Once, a long time ago, there lived a peasant. He always observed St. Nicholas’ day, but never, never, that of St. Elias; he even worked on it."

memes.label: "The Princess to Be Kissed at a Charge"
memes.description: "We still say that we are clever, but our elders go and quarrel with us and say, “No, we had more sense than you."

memes.label: "The Wood Sprite"
memes.description: "One day the daughter of a pope, without asking leave of her mother or her father, went for a walk into the wood, and utterly lost her way. Three years went by."

memes.label: "The Realms of Copper, Silver and Gold"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was an old man and his old wife, and they had three sons."

memes.label: "Chufíl-Fílyushka"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there were three brothers in a family; the eldest was called the Ram, the second the Goat, and the third and youngest Chufíl-Fílyushka."

memes.label: "Donotknow"
memes.description: "Here begins the tale of a grey horse, a chestnut horse and of the wise fallow-bay."

memes.label: "The Sea Tsar and Vasilísa the Wise"
memes.description: "Once a Tsar lived with his Tsarítsa beyond thrice-nine lands in the thrice-tenth kingdom. He liked to go hunting and shooting the wild beasts."

memes.label: "The Animals’ Winter Quarters"
memes.description: "Once an Ox was wandering in the wood, and a Ram met him. “Where are you going, Ram?” asked the Ox."

memes.label: "The Story of the Brave and Doughty Knight Ilyá Múromets and the Nightingale Robber"
memes.description: "Once in the famous city of Múrom48 in the village of Karachárovo, a peasant lived who was called Iván Timoféyevich; he had one beloved son, Ilyá Múromets."

memes.label: "Nikíta the Tanner"
memes.description: "One day, somewhere near Kíev, a dragon appeared, who demanded heavy tribute from the people."

memes.label: "The Singing-Tree and the Speaking-Bird"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a very inquisitive King who spent all his time eavesdropping at the window."

memes.label: "At the Behest of the Pike"
memes.description: "Once there lived a poor peasant; and, however much he might toil and moil, he got nothing out of it."

memes.label: "The Journey to Jerusalem"
memes.description: "An archimandrite one day got up for matins; and, whilst laving his hands, saw an unclean spirit in the Holy Water, seized him and crossed him."

memes.label: "Vazúza and Vólga"
memes.description: "The Vólga and the Vazúza had a long argument whether who was the wiser and the stronger and the more honourable of the two."

memes.label: "The Enchanted Tsarévich"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a merchant who had three daughters: it so happened he had one day to go to strange countries to buy wares, and so he asked his daughters, “What shall ."

memes.label: "The Snake Princess"
memes.description: "A Cossack was going on his road and way, and he arrived in the sleepy forest, and in that forest, in a glade, stood a hayrick."

memes.label: "Beer and Bread"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom, in a certain State, there once lived a rich peasant, and he had much money and bread; he used to lend money on interest to the poor husbandmen of his villa."

memes.label: "Sorrow"
memes.description: "Once upon a time, in a wretched village, there lived two peasants, who were own brothers. One was poor, however, and the other rich."

memes.label: "Iváshko and the Wise Woman"
memes.description: "Once there lived an old man and an old dame, and they only had one little son, and you can’t imagine how they loved him."

memes.label: "Never-Wash"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was a soldier who had served through three campaigns, but had never earned as much as an addled egg, and was then put on the retired list."

memes.label: "Christ and the Geese"
memes.description: "One day St. Peter and Christ were out walking together. St."

memes.label: "Christ and Folksongs"
memes.description: "One day Christ and St. Peter were walking about the earth and came to a village. In one house folks were singing so finely that Christ stayed to listen, whilst St."

memes.label: "The Devil in the Dough-Pan"
memes.description: "Once a woman was kneading bread, but had forgotten to say the blessing. So the demon, Potánka,57 ran up and sat down in it."

memes.label: "The Sun, the Moon, and Crow Crowson"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there was an old man and an old woman who had three daughters."

memes.label: "The Legless Knight and the Blind Knight"
memes.description: "In a certain kingdom in a certain land a Tsar and his Tsarítsa lived."

memes.label: "A Cure for Story-Telling"
memes.description: "There was once a porter in the world: he had a wife who was passionately fond of stories, and she would only let people come and visit her who could tell stories."

memes.label: "Notes"
memes.description: "本结构包含以下章节："

memes.label: "General Notes"
memes.description: "- Alyósha Popóvich. One of the great knights at the court of Prince Vladímir."

memes.label: "The Crystal Apple and the Silver Saucer"
memes.description: "- There is a strong Celtic flavour about this episode. Cf. The Twa Sisters o’ Binnorie."

memes.label: "The Dream. Notes"
memes.description: "- The *izbá*, or hut, always has a *dvor* or courtyard, access to which is gained through double gates as well as through a postern."

memes.label: "Midnight Dance. General Notes to This Story"
memes.description: "- The underworld is the home of magic. This charm, to be said by a soldier going to the wars, may be of interest."

memes.label: Alyósha Popóvich
memes.description: The youngest of the three great bogatyrs of the Kievan cycle — a knight born of a priest, companion to Ilyá Múromets and blood-brother to Dobrýnya Nikítich, serving Prince Vladímir

memes.label: Animal Helpers in Folklore
memes.description: The motif of magical animal helpers in Russian folktales — the grey Wolf, the speaking bird, the horse of power, and other creatures that aid the hero

memes.label: Bába Yagá
memes.description: The cannibal witch of Russian folklore who lives in a hut on cocks' legs in the deep forest, rides the wind in a mortar, and serves as both devourer and threshold guardian

memes.label: Bryánsk Forest
memes.description: The ancient, impenetrable forest of the Bryánsk region — legendary home of magic in Russian folklore, where the aspen grows and Death was imprisoned

memes.label: Danílo the Unfortunate
memes.description: A noble henchman at Prince Vladímir's court in Kíev, who rises from misfortune through the magical aid of the Swan-bird maiden

memes.label: Death in Russian Folklore
memes.description: The personification and narrative role of Death in Russian folktales, from the water of Life and Death to Koshchéy the Deathless

memes.label: Dobrýnya Nikítich
memes.description: One of the great knight-bogatyrs of the Kievan cycle — dragon-slayer, diplomat, and blood-brother to Alyósha Popóvich, serving Prince Vladímir in the byliny tradition

memes.label: "Donotknow"
memes.description: "Donotknow is the disguise identity of Iván the merchant's son in Russian folklore, who hides his heroic nature beneath an oxhide and a bladder while secretly defending a kingdom from invasion with the aid of a magical horse."

memes.label: Dragon in Russian Folklore
memes.description: The dragon (zmey) in Russian folktales — the multi-headed serpent that demands tribute, guards realms, and is slain by the bogatyr hero

memes.label: Egóri the Brave
memes.description: A divine messenger in Russian folklore who carries God's instructions to men, identified with the folkloric counterpart of St. George the Dragon-slayer.

memes.label: Eléna the Fair
memes.description: The fair princess from a distant kingdom across thrice-nine lands who becomes the bride of Iván Tsarévich after being abducted by the grey Wolf and surviving a treacherous murder plot

memes.label: The Firebird
memes.description: The luminous bird of Russian folklore whose golden feathers light the darkness — simultaneously thief, captive, guide, and the ultimate quest object

memes.label: The Grey Wolf
memes.description: The magical grey Wolf of Russian folklore — supernatural helper to Iván Tsarévich who carries the hero across thrice-nine lands, negotiates with cosmic beings, and restores the dead to life

memes.label: Ilyá Múromets
memes.description: The greatest of the Russian bogatyrí — the knight from Murom who gained superhuman strength after thirty years as an invalid and became the defender of Kíev and the Russian land

memes.label: Iván Tsarévich
memes.description: The archetypal hero of Russian folktales — the youngest tsar's son who undertakes quests across thrice-nine lands to win the Firebird, the golden-maned horse, and the fair princess

memes.label: Ivánushka
memes.description: The youngest-son fool hero of Russian folktales — seemingly simple Ivánushka who succeeds through kindness and obedience where his cleverer brothers fail, guided by a mysterious old man who bestows magical knowledge

memes.label: "Iváshko Zapéchnik"
memes.description: "Iváshko Zapéchnik is the youngest of three brothers in Russian folklore, a fool-hero who succeeds where his elders fail, descending into the underground realms of copper, silver, and gold to rescue three captive maidens."

memes.label: Katomá
memes.description: The marvellous servant with the oaken-cap who serves as Iván Tsarévich's protector, counsellor, and supernatural helper — defeats magical foes and overcomes death itself

memes.label: Kíev in Folklore
memes.description: The city of Kíev as it appears in Russian folktales — the court of Prince Vladímir, home of the bogatyrí, and the center of the civilized world from which heroes set forth

memes.label: Koshchéy the Deathless
memes.description: The great baleful magician of Russian folktales whose soul is hidden outside his body — the archetypal immortal villain who kidnaps the hero's wife and must be defeated by discovering the secret of his death

memes.label: Koshchéy the Deathless
memes.description: The great baleful magician of Russian folklore whose soul is hidden in an egg far away — the archetypal villain who cannot die until his external soul is found and destroyed

memes.label: Magic in Russian Folklore
memes.description: The role and manifestations of magic in Russian folktales, from pre-Christian Slavic paganism through the Afanasyev collection

memes.label: Magic Mirror
memes.description: The magic mirror of Russian folktales — a scrying device through which its possessor can see events happening anywhere in the world

memes.label: Magical Fruits in Folklore
memes.description: Magical fruits in Russian folktales — the silver saucer and crystal apple, golden apples of the Firebird's desire, and fruits of youth

memes.label: Magical Horse
memes.description: The enchanted horse of Russian folklore — a supernatural steed restrained by twelve chains, capable of flight, and loyal only to the worthy hero

memes.label: "Mark the Rich"
memes.description: "Mark the Rich is a wealthy merchant in Russian folklore whose arrogance and refusal of charity lead him to attempt the murder of a prophesied rival, only to fall victim to his own traps through divine intervention."

memes.label: Márya Moryévna
memes.description: The warrior princess of Russian folklore — a powerful female ruler who captures Koshchéy the Deathless and marries Iván Tsarévich, triggering the great pursuit across thrice-nine lands

memes.label: Nightingale Robber
memes.description: A monstrous robber in Russian folklore with a deadly supernatural whistle, defeated by the bogatyr Ilyá Múromets and brought before Prince Vladímir in Kíev.

memes.label: Nikíta the Tanner
memes.description: A humble tanner of Kíev in Russian folklore who defeats a dragon that had been terrorizing the land, tricks it into ploughing a furrow from Kíev to the Caspian Sea, and then slays it — demanding no reward for his deed.

memes.label: Prince Vladímir
memes.description: The ruler of Kíev in the age of the bogatyrí — the prince at whose court Ilyá Múromets and Alyósha Popóvich serve

memes.label: Prophetic Dreams in Folklore
memes.description: The role of dreams as prophetic visions and narrative devices in Russian folktales

memes.label: Reward and Punishment
memes.description: The moral economy governing Russian folktale outcomes — the good rewarded, the wicked punished, the clever elevated

memes.label: Slavic Paganism
memes.description: The pre-Christian Slavic belief system that underlies the supernatural elements of Russian folktales

memes.label: Svyatogór
memes.description: The giant bogatyr of the Holy Mountains — a legendary knight of immense stature who carries his wife in his pocket, meets Ilyá Múromets on the road, and is ultimately sealed in a tomb from which he cannot rise

memes.label: The Aspen Tree in Folklore
memes.description: The significance of the aspen tree in Russian folklore — the cursed tree on which Judas hanged himself, associated with magic, wizardry, and death

memes.label: The Church in Russian Folklore
memes.description: The role of the Orthodox Church, priests, and Christian elements as they appear in Russian folktales after the Christianization of 988 CE

memes.label: The Firebird Motif
memes.description: The Firebird (Zhar-ptitsa) as a narrative motif — the luminous bird whose feathers illuminate the darkness and whose pursuit drives the hero's quest

memes.label: The Fool in Russian Folklore
memes.description: The figure of the fool or simpleton in Russian tales — often the youngest son, seemingly stupid, who proves wiser than the clever

memes.label: The Hero's Quest
memes.description: The narrative structure of the hero's journey in Russian folktales — the quest across thrice-nine lands to obtain a magical object, rescue a princess, or defeat a deathless villain

memes.label: The Isle of Buyán
memes.description: The mythical island of Russian folklore — a fairy isle comparable to the Irish Tír na nÓg, home to the oak where Koshchéy's soul-egg is hidden

memes.label: The Nosebag of Death
memes.description: The humble soldier's nosebag that became the prison of Death herself — a mundane object elevated to cosmic significance through the soldier's trickery

memes.label: "The Sea Tsar"
memes.description: "The Sea Tsar is a mythological ruler of the underwater kingdom in Russian folklore, father of Vasilísa the Wise, who sets impossible tasks for mortal heroes and commands the powers of the deep."

memes.label: The Self-Playing Gusli
memes.description: The *gusli-samogudy* — the self-playing psaltery of Russian folklore, a magical stringed instrument that plays by itself, compelling listeners to dance or sleep

memes.label: The Silver Saucer and Crystal Apple
memes.description: The enchanted saucer and apple of Russian folklore — when the crystal apple is rolled on the silver saucer, it reveals all the cities, seas, mountains, and forests of the entire world

memes.label: The Soldier Hero
memes.description: The discharged soldier as hero archetype in Russian folktales — the veteran who uses wits, courage, and twenty-five years of army experience to outsmart Death, the Devil, and even the Tsar

memes.label: The Soul in Russian Folklore
memes.description: The concept of the external or hidden soul in Russian folktales, most famously embodied in Koshchéy the Deathless

memes.label: The Thrice-Tenth Realm
memes.description: The thrice-tenth realm — the faraway kingdom beyond thrice-nine lands where the Firebird, the golden-maned horse, and the fair princess await

memes.label: The Underworld
memes.description: The underworld in Russian folklore — the subterranean home of magic, where the realms of copper, silver, and gold lie, and from which heroes must ascend

memes.label: The Water of Life and Death
memes.description: The paired magical waters that respectively reunite the dead body and restore life — a distinctive motif of Russian folktales

memes.label: Thrice-Nine Lands
memes.description: The formulaic opening and mythic geography of Russian folktales — "beyond thrice-nine lands in the thrice-tenth realm"

memes.label: Tsar Bárkhat
memes.description: A tsar in Russian folklore who repeatedly attempts and fails to determine whether the cross-dressing Vasilísa Vasílyevna is a man or a woman, and is outwitted each time.

memes.label: Túgarin Zmyéyevich
memes.description: A winged adversary in the Kíev cycle of Russian byliny, identified as the Serpent's Son, who duels Alyósha Popóvich at the court of Prince Vladímir and is slain in the open field.

memes.label: Vasíli Tsarévich
memes.description: The middle Tsarévich — envious second son of Tsar Výslav Andrónovich who conspires with his elder brother Dmítri to murder [[ivan-tsarevich|Iván Tsarévich]] and seize the Firebird, the golden-maned horse, and Princess Eléna the Fair

memes.label: Vasilísa the Fair
memes.description: The archetypal heroine of Russian folktales — the beautiful and wise young woman who triumphs over the witch Bába Yagá through courage, skill, and the blessing of her dead mother

memes.label: "Zamorýshek"
memes.description: "Zamorýshek is a miraculous child in Russian folklore, born from an egg as the weakest of forty-one brothers, who proves himself the cleverest and most resourceful, capturing a magical mare and saving his siblings from Bába Yagá."
